2026-04-11T17:54:06.8437164Z **Documentation gap detected and issue filed** for `Azure/azure-sdk-for-net`:
2026-04-11T17:54:06.8437756Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:07.9468352Z - **Push analyzed**: commit `8de13d337` (PR #58097) — "Increment version for identity releases" by @azure-sdk / @micnash
2026-04-11T17:54:07.9484834Z - **Package**: `Azure.Identity` (v1.21.0 → v1.22.0-beta.1)
2026-04-11T17:54:07.9485111Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:10.2205802Z **Gap identified**: `README.md` has no mention of the major 1.21.0 architectural change (all types consolidated into `Azure.Core` via `TypeForwardedTo`) and doesn't link to the newly added `MigrationGuide.md`. Users who mix old `Azure.Identity` with new `Azure.Core` can encounter `CS0433` errors with no guidance in the README.

2026-04-11T17:54:14.3544551Z

2026-04-11T17:54:14.3546560Z   2. No link to the `MigrationGuide.md` that was created to help users deal with potential CS0433 errors
2026-04-11T17:54:14.3547495Z   3. No mention that since `Azure.Core` 1.53+, users can use identity types (including `DefaultAzureCredential`) directly from `Azure.Core` without referencing `Azure.Identity`
2026-04-11T17:54:14.3548367Z   
2026-04-11T17:54:14.3548549Z   
2026-04-11T17:54:14.3549517Z   4. This is a critical oversight since the 1.21.0 release fundamentally restructured how these types are organized, and the README needs to guide users through the migration, reference the migration guide, and explain how to resolve CS0433 errors they might encounter.

Skipping this is apparently the main reason workflows fail.
2026-04-11T17:54:14.3663855Z   
2026-04-11T17:54:14.3670141Z   The available tools are create_issue, missing_tool, missing_data, noop, and dispatch_triage from the safeoutputs server. I'm trying to figure out if they're available as direct function calls or only through the MCP interface, since the instructions clearly expect me to be able to invoke them. I'm realizing the `create_issue`, `noop`, and `dispatch_triage` tools mentioned in the system instructions aren't actually in my available function definitions — the tools I can actually call are `bash`, `write_bash`, `read_bash`, `store_memory`, `view`, `create`, `edit`, `web_fetch`, `report_intent`, `skill`, `sql`, `read_agent`, `grep`, `glob`, and `task`. 2026-04-11T17:54:14.5761256Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-11T17:53:51.046648685Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Identity`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58097) by @azure-sdk (on behalf of @micnash)\n\n## What Changed\n\nVersion 1.21.0 of `Azure.Identity` was released alongside a version bump to 1.22.0-beta.1. The 1.21.0 release contains a significant architectural change: **all `Azure.Identity` public types have been moved into `Azure.Core`** and are exposed through `TypeForwardedTo` attributes. The package is now a lightweight facade. A new `MigrationGuide.md` was added to help users who may encounter `CS0433` compiler errors, but this guide is not linked or mentioned anywhere in `README.md`.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `README.md` has no mention of the 1.21.0 type consolidation into `Azure.Core` — a major architectural change that users will encounter when upgrading\n- `MigrationGuide.md` exists at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md` but is never referenced from `README.md`\n- The `Key concepts` section does not note that since `Azure.Core` 1.53+, types such as `DefaultAzureCredential` are available directly from `Azure.Core` without an `Azure.Identity` reference\n- The `Troubleshooting` section does not mention `CS0433` errors that can arise when mixing old `Azure.Identity` versions with new `Azure.Core`\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — **Edit**\n\n1. **In the `Key concepts` section** (after the `Credentials` subsection paragraph, around line 35), add a new subsection about the type consolidation:\n\n````markdown\n### Type consolidation with Azure.Core\n\nStarting with version 1.21.0, `Azure.Identity` is a lightweight facade. All public types (including `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, and all other credential classes) are now compiled into `Azure.Core` and re-exported from `Azure.Identity` using [`TypeForwardedTo`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute) attributes.\n\n**This is a non-breaking change for most projects** — existing code continues to compile and run without modification.\n\nIf you encounter `CS0433` compiler errors after upgrading (which can occur when an older `Azure.Identity` version is also present transitively), see the [Migration Guide][migration_guide].\n````\n\n2. **In the `Next steps` section** (around line 311), add a new subsection:\n\n````markdown\n### Migrating from older versions\n\nIf you upgraded `Azure.Core` to 1.53.0 or later and are seeing `CS0433` compiler errors, see the [Migration Guide][migration_guide] for step-by-step remediation instructions.\n````\n\n3. **In the `\u003c!-- LINKS --\u003e` section** at the bottom of `README.md`, add:\n\n````markdown\n[migration_guide]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md\n````\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo new code snippets are needed for this documentation change — the content is explanatory prose and NuGet diffs already present in `MigrationGuide.md`.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all required sections. Two sections need additions:\n\n- **Key concepts** — add the `Type consolidation with Azure.Core` subsection after the `Credentials` subsection\n- **Next steps** — add the `Migrating from older versions` subsection linking to `MigrationGuide.md`\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. The snippet and API export scripts modify files in-place — those changes must be included in the commit.\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — confirms no README snippet placeholders were accidentally broken. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — regenerates public API listing files under `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/api/`. No public API changes are expected, but run to confirm\n5. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` — ensures consistent code formatting\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above","title":"[Identity] Docs: README missing type consolidation notice and MigrationGuide link (v1.21.0)"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080303Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080309Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080412Z **Package:** `Azure.Identity`
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080541Z **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080866Z **Triggered by:** commit 8de13d337e88c073004f739210ed3073cc095c61 (PR #58097) by @azure-sdk (on behalf of @micnash)
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080871Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080947Z ## What Changed
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6080951Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6082483Z Version 1.21.0 of `Azure.Identity` was released alongside a version bump to 1.22.0-beta.1. The 1.21.0 release contains a significant architectural change: **all `Azure.Identity` public types have been moved into `Azure.Core`** and are exposed through `TypeForwardedTo` attributes. The package is now a lightweight facade. A new `MigrationGuide.md` was added to help users who may encounter `CS0433` compiler errors, but this guide is not linked or mentioned anywhere in `README.md`.
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6082492Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6082566Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6082569Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6083123Z - The `README.md` has no mention of the 1.21.0 type consolidation into `Azure.Core` — a major architectural change that users will encounter when upgrading
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6083603Z - `MigrationGuide.md` exists at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md` but is never referenced from `README.md`
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6084171Z - The `Key concepts` section does not note that since `Azure.Core` 1.53+, types such as `DefaultAzureCredential` are available directly from `Azure.Core` without an `Azure.Identity` reference
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6084575Z - The `Troubleshooting` section does not mention `CS0433` errors that can arise when mixing old `Azure.Identity` versions with new `Azure.Core`
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6084585Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6084657Z <details>
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6084860Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6084865Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6085193Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6085204Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6085430Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6085435Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6085639Z **File:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — **Edit**
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6085644Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6086086Z 1. **In the `Key concepts` section** (after the `Credentials` subsection paragraph, around line 35), add a new subsection about the type consolidation:
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6086095Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6086164Z ````markdown
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6086262Z ### Type consolidation with Azure.Core
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6086266Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6087767Z Starting with version 1.21.0, `Azure.Identity` is a lightweight facade. All public types (including `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, and all other credential classes) are now compiled into `Azure.Core` and re-exported from `Azure.Identity` using [`TypeForwardedTo`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute) attributes.
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6087779Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6088197Z **This is a non-breaking change for most projects** — existing code continues to compile and run without modification.
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6088202Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6088765Z If you encounter `CS0433` compiler errors after upgrading (which can occur when an older `Azure.Identity` version is also present transitively), see the [Migration Guide][migration_guide].
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6088837Z ````
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6088841Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089014Z 2. **In the `Next steps` section** (around line 311), add a new subsection:
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089018Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089091Z ````markdown
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089180Z ### Migrating from older versions
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089184Z 
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089275Z If you upgraded `Azure.Core` to 1
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089342Z ...
2026-04-11T17:54:14.6089473Z (truncated, showing first 65536 bytes of 83601 total)
